title: Rust lichen-coap: CoapBuilder content_format uses invalid byte slicing
description: In rust/lichen-coap/src/codec.rs at the content_format() method, the code creates 'let bytes = format.to_be_bytes()' (a [u8; 2]) then slices it as &bytes[2..2] for format==0, &bytes[1..2] for 1..=0xFF, and &bytes[0..2] otherwise. The slice &bytes[2..2] for format==0 is problematic: a [u8; 2] array only has indices 0 and 1, so bytes[2..2] is out of bounds. This will panic at runtime when encoding Content-Format 0.
close_reason: False positive: &bytes[2..2] is a valid empty slice at the end of a [u8; 2] array. Rust allows slicing from len..len to produce an empty slice.
